name: TAO Retest Hold Derisk v1
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing retest-and-hold continuation that de-risks by time if expansion fails to appear quickly. In a fully choppy regime, the value is not in chasing trend; it is in participating only after a hold and cutting exposure quickly if no extension appears.
edge_mechanism: retest hold continuation with time-based derisk
invalidation_condition: Abort if retest-hold entries remain too sparse, if holds fail to continue often enough, or if time-based de-risking cannot protect against dead-money persistence.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price reclaims fast value, retests without losing it, and continues only if the retest is accepted. If expansion does not appear quickly, exposure is cut rather than waiting indefinitely.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: This combines a broadly available retest grammar with a realistic failure response. In chop, de-risking speed can matter more than aiming for a large fixed target.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the right structurally different lane because retest behavior is expressive and the derisking logic has room to matter on a volatile asset.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If TAO remains purely mean-reverting, the retest may hold briefly but never deliver enough continuation for the idea to work.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries engage but expansion rarely arrives, keep the entry and tighten the time-based derisk rule further.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; retest hold and participation check are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed with broad hold-zone logic instead of exact candle choreography

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.15 * ATR_14 by selling 20%
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.15 * ATR_14 by covering 20%
